{"society_power": 1.5, "state_power": 9.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Xi's consolidation, digital surveillance state, 709 crackdown", "reasoning": "The surveillance state, Xi's centralization, abolition of term limits, and Xinjiang camps pushed state capacity toward near-total penetration, while the 709 crackdown and NGO/media controls crushed the nascent rights movement despite latent middle-class potential and Hong Kong's exceptional (mainland-isolated) mobilization."}
